name: Byzantine Dolmathes (Stuffed Grapeleaves)
recipeIngredient: ¼ cup pine nuts or walnuts
recipeInstructions:
If using canned grape leaves, rinse them in a basin of cold water. For fresh leaves, pour boiling water over them in a bowl and drain.
Spread 5-6 leaves on a flat surface, stem side up. Snip the stem with kitchen shears.
MAKE FILLING: Heat oil in a large frying pan. Fry meat, onions, and garlic on medium heat for 5 minutes. Add remaining ingredients and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer and cook until water is absorbed. Set aside to cool slightly.
Assemble dolmathes:
1. Place about one teaspoon of filling near the stem.
2. Fold the left side towards the center, then fold the right side towards the center (they may not meet).
3. Pick up the stem end of the leaf and tuck in the filling. Roll away from you to form an oblong roll like a sausage.
4. Line the bottom of a large skillet with four leaves. Arrange each roll so the tucked end is on the bottom, snugly fitting one next to the other until all leaves (except three) are used.
5. Place remaining leaves flat on top of the rolls and cover with a dish.
Cook:
1. Add water and lemon juice to the skillet. Bring to a boil.
2. Cover and reduce heat to simmer for 45 minutes.
3. Remove from heat, then make Egg and Lemon Sauce (see below) and stir into the broth immediately or serve without sauce as an appetizer or hot entree.
Egg and Lemon Sauce:
1. Beat eggs until thick and light yellow, at least 5 minutes with an electric beater, or 10-15 minutes by hand.
2. Slowly add lemon juice while beating.
3. Mix in the hot broth quickly to avoid curdling the eggs. Cook over very low heat until thickened.
From: "The Complete Greek Cookbook" by Theresa Karas Yianilos. Avenel Books, New York.
